violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the coffee refrigerator have an accumulation of food/beverage residue. Staff began cleaning unit during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2025

The outside dumpster is not covered. Inspector observed covers were jammed/stuck on wood railing, leaving establishment unable to close receptacle. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. PIC stated this happened earlier today when container was emptied. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Sliced tomatoes were observed held at 52-55 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Inspector observed the tomatoes were in a plastic container greater than the fill line. Per PIC, the tomatoes taken out of the unit and recently returned to refrigeration. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC took tomatoes out of container and placed directly into prep unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024

Inspector observed a clean and sanitized blender with pooled water. Utensils and equipment must be allowed to air dry before storage. PIC opened lid to facilitate drying. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024

Utensils in the storage crock are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. PIC took utensils to 3-bay sink to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
